perturbation.original_text: 1.01 CONTRACTORS. Affiliate shall use its best efforts to market and sell to commercial businesses the Services of Network 1 and Network 1's subsidiaries and to locate individuals who are willing and capable of acting as Contractors of Network 1 and Affiliate subject to the approval of all such individuals by Network 1 as set out in Section 2.01 [CONTRACTORS]. All such Contractors must process Merchant applications and transactions exclusively through Network 1. Network 1 consents to waive said exclusivity requirement with respect to specific Merchants in the event Network 1 (i) is unable to process for such specific Merchant, and (ii) the declined Merchant is not accepted for processing by a provider that Network 1 designates for specific Merchant's that are declined by Network 1 ("B Bank Source"). Affiliate shall disclose to Network 1 all information known to Affiliate with respect to the background, character, employment history, business experience and other information regarding each prospective Contractor relevant to Network 1's determination whether to accept or not accept each prospective Contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.01 CONTRACTORS. Affiliate shall use reasonable efforts to market and sell to commercial businesses the Services of Network 1 and Network 1's subsidiaries and to locate individuals who are willing and capable of acting as Contractors of Network 1 and Affiliate subject to the approval of all such individuals by Network 1 as set out in Section 2.01 [CONTRACTORS]. Contractors located by the Affiliate are encouraged, but not required, to process Merchant applications and transactions through Network 1 whenever feasible. Network 1 consents to waive said exclusivity requirement with respect to specific Merchants in the event Network 1 (i) is unable to process for such specific Merchant, and (ii) the declined Merchant is not accepted for processing by a provider that Network 1 designates for specific Merchant's that are declined by Network 1 ("B Bank Source"). Affiliate shall disclose to Network 1 all information known to Affiliate with respect to the background, character, employment history, business experience and other information regarding each prospective Contractor relevant to Network 1's determination whether to accept or not accept each prospective Contractor.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'best efforts' to 'reasonable efforts' and changing the mandatory requirement for Contractors to process exclusively through Network 1 to 'encouraged, but not required', the contract now contains ambiguity regarding the contractor's obligations. This contradicts Section 2.01 [CONTRACTORS] which outlines the rights of Network 1 regarding contractors and assumes an exclusive arrangement. The altered wording makes the enforcement of exclusivity unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.01 CONTRACTORS. Network 1 shall have the right, at its discretion, to accept, not accept, terminate or otherwise deal with any individuals located by Affiliate pursuant to Section 1.01 [CONTRACTORS]. All individuals identified by Affiliate shall be subject to the rules imposed by Network 1, VISA, MasterCard, NACHA, and the Member bank ("Member") utilized by Network 1, including rules pertaining to qualification disqualification, conduct and otherwise, and shall agree to enter into, and shall in fact enter into, a three party Contractor Agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit 1 as amended from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates that contractors process exclusively through Network 1. The changed text states contractors are merely 'encouraged, but not required' to do so. This directly contradicts section 2.01, which grants Network 1 the right to 'deal with any individuals located by Affiliate' and subjects those individuals to Network 1's rules. The changed text weakens Network 1's control and creates ambiguity about contractor obligations, directly conflicting with Network 1's rights as outlined in section 2.01, as the contractor might not be bound by the rules if they are not processing exclusively through Network 1.

perturbation.original_text: 3.01 AMOUNT. As compensation for Affiliate's services hereunder, Network 1, or an affiliate, shall pay to Affiliate the following (the "Affiliate's Fee"): A. The surplus funding amount after costs noted in Exhibit A based on all Merchant applications obtained for Equipment and Products sold or leased by Affiliate or Contractors located by Affiliate pursuant to Section 1.01 [CONTRACTORS]. Affiliate shall receive the buy rate with revenue share as noted in Exhibit A. B. Affiliate acknowledges and agrees that all merchants and subscribing businesses referred by Affiliate are a party to a contract with Network 1 and it's Member bank and that Network 1 and the Member bank has a direct contractual relationship with the merchants and subscribing businesses. Network 1 acknowledges and agrees that Affiliate has certain rights to residual revenue as specifically defined in this agreement. Affiliate acknowledges and agrees that it has no claims or rights of ownership over Merchants and subscribing businesses, Unless ISO agreement is consummated or Network 1 does not approve Affiliate or Network 1 cancels Affiliate and that it is a service provider to a customer of Network 1 (namely Merchants and subscribing businesses). In such case, Affiliate shall have the right to Transfer accounts in accordance with Section 2.03 (b) [RETENTION OF CONTRACTORS] and 2.03 (c). The Affiliate's Fees outlined in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein shall be subject to modification by Network 1, at its discretion, upon the giving of thirty-days (30) prior written notification.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.01 AMOUNT. As compensation for Affiliate's services hereunder, Network 1, or an affiliate, shall pay to Affiliate the following (the "Affiliate's Fee"): A. The surplus funding amount after costs noted in Exhibit A based on all Merchant applications obtained for Equipment and Products sold or leased by Affiliate or Contractors located by Affiliate pursuant to Section 1.01 [CONTRACTORS]. Affiliate shall receive the buy rate with revenue share as noted in Exhibit A. B. Affiliate acknowledges and agrees that all merchants and subscribing businesses referred by Affiliate are a party to a contract with Network 1 and it's Member bank and that Network 1 and the Member bank has a direct contractual relationship with the merchants and subscribing businesses. Network 1 acknowledges and agrees that Affiliate may have certain rights to residual revenue as defined by Network 1. Affiliate acknowledges and agrees that it has no claims or rights of ownership over Merchants and subscribing businesses and that it is a service provider to a customer of Network 1 (namely Merchants and subscribing businesses). In such case, Affiliate shall have the right to Transfer accounts in accordance with Section 2.03 (b) [RETENTION OF CONTRACTORS] and 2.03 (c). The Affiliate's Fees outlined in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein shall be subject to modification by Network 1, at its discretion, upon the giving of thirty-days (30) prior written notification.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Affiliate has certain rights to residual revenue as specifically defined in this agreement' to 'Affiliate may have certain rights to residual revenue as defined by Network 1' and removing the exception for 'Unless ISO agreement is consummated or Network 1 does not approve Affiliate or Network 1 cancels Affiliate', this introduces ambiguity regarding residual revenue rights. This contradicts the implicit understanding that Affiliate would have previously defined and agreed-upon rights. The power to unilaterally define those rights shifts entirely to Network 1, making Section 5.03, which discusses the effect of termination on Affiliate's fees, less enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.03 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon termination of this Agreement, except in the event of termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]A, Network 1 shall continue to pay to the Affiliate the Affiliate's Fee described in Section 3.01 [AMOUNT]B for so long as Network 1 continues to process transactions of the Merchants in accordance with Schedule B attached hereto and Affiliate continues to maintain the merchant accounts, provided, however, that no such Affiliate's Fees shall be earned or paid for any calendar month in which the Affiliate's Fee does not exceed $500. Upon termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate, or termination for cause pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]D all Affiliates fees shall cease to be accrued and paid immediately upon the occurrence of said event. Additionally, upon termination of this contract for any reason, all merchants recruited by Affiliate on behalf of Network 1 for any product offered through Network 1, Affiliate shall not approach, rewrite, pursue, or contract with any current client for the purpose of obtaining said client as a new customer for Affiliate or any competing entity the Affiliate may be in contract with. Such restriction shall also apply to Affiliate's past, current and future officers, directors, sales representatives, and Contractors. If terminated for cause Affiliate is responsible for any expense incurred by Network 1 for investigating incidents, attorney fees, fines, and administration expense.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'Affiliate has certain rights to residual revenue as specifically defined in this agreement' with 'Affiliate may have certain rights to residual revenue as defined by Network 1'. This change, coupled with the removal of the exception clause, shifts the power to define residual revenue rights entirely to Network 1. This directly contradicts the original implication that the Affiliate had defined and agreed-upon rights to residual revenue. The contradicted text, Section 5.03, states that Network 1 shall continue to pay the Affiliate's Fee (which includes residual revenue) upon termination under certain conditions. However, if Network 1 unilaterally defines these rights, it could effectively eliminate or significantly reduce the Affiliate's residual revenue upon termination, thereby rendering Section 5.03 less enforceable and creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 5.01 TERM. The term ("Term") of this Agreement shall be for one hundred eighty days (180) from the date set forth below unless Network 1 or Visa or MasterCard or Harris Bank doesn't approve Affiliate's ISO application, in which case, the Term will be 3 years. This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless terminated by either party by providing the other with 30 days written notice that this Agreement will not be renewed or Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.01 TERM. The term ("Term") of this Agreement shall be for one hundred eighty days (180) from the date set forth below unless Network 1 or Visa or MasterCard or Harris Bank doesn't approve Affiliate's ISO application, in which case, the Term could be 3 years. This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless terminated by either party by providing the other with 30 days written notice that this Agreement will not be renewed or Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'the Term will be 3 years' to 'the Term could be 3 years', the definite term extension upon failure of ISO approval is made uncertain. This directly contradicts the later part of the same section regarding automatic termination upon the Affiliate entering into a Processing and ISO Sponsorship agreement. If the term extension is not guaranteed, it becomes unclear whether that termination clause applies in the situation where the ISO application was initially unapproved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.01 TERM. The term ("Term") of this Agreement shall be for one hundred eighty days (180) from the date set forth below unless Network 1 or Visa or MasterCard or Harris Bank doesn't approve Affiliate's ISO application, in which case, the Term could be 3 years. This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless terminated by either party by providing the other with 30 days written notice that this Agreement will not be renewed or Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'the Term will be 3 years' to 'the Term could be 3 years', introducing uncertainty about the term extension upon the failure of ISO approval. This directly contradicts the portion of the same section which states 'Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements.' because the changed text makes the term length less certain. This uncertainty creates an ambiguity concerning whether the automatic termination clause applies when the ISO application wasn't initially approved. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction, where key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently, making interpretation unclear.
